No words were deemed unfixable gibberish or single-character entries, so none were entirely discarded. A scholarly guess was made for 'ወድው', correcting it to 'ወደው' and deriving its root from 'መውደድ' (to love), interpreting it as 'lovingly' or 'having loved'. Proper nouns like 'ፐሮፒላክሲስ' and 'አሊፂያ' were retained as nouns. summary: The input batch was processed through 8 scholarly protocols. 'ኢንዘሃውስ' (in-house) and 'ዌዘሬልን' (Wetherell) were discarded under Protocol 2 (Nonsense Removal) as they are foreign loanwords or proper nouns that do not serve as general Amharic dictionary entries. 'ማሶገድ' was visually repaired to 'ማስወገድ' for correct spelling. 'ኣባቶችና' was corrected to 'አባቶችና' and 'እንዲፅፍና' to 'እንዲጽፍና' under Protocol 3 (Visual Repair). 'የመስሥያ' was identified as a likely OCR error and corrected to 'መስሪያ' (office/instrument) under Protocol 3, representing a scholarly guess based on morphological plausibility and common Amharic vocabulary. All remaining words were successfully lemmatized, translated, and tagged with their major Part of Speech. No words were split, and all included words meet the minimum two-character length requirement.

The input batch underwent rigorous processing according to the 8 scholarly protocols. Two merged words were identified and split: 'በደተደረገ' into 'በደል' and 'ተደረገ', and 'ይገጥማልበኢትዮጵያ' into 'ይገጥማል' and 'በኢትዮጵያ'. Two foreign transliterations, 'ሰምቲንግ' (English 'something') and 'ኤልአረቢ' (Arabic 'Al-Arabi'), were discarded as they do not represent native Amharic lexical entries suitable for a dictionary. A visual OCR confusion was corrected for 'በዝኒ', which was interpreted and lemmatized as 'በዚህ' (root: 'ይህ'). A scholarly guess was made for 'ስንፈስክ', which is not a standard Amharic root; it was interpreted as a potential OCR error for a verb meaning 'to scatter' or 'to spread', and thus lemmatized to 'መበተን'. All words were stripped of punctuation and transformed to their global citation forms (infinitive for verbs, base form for nouns/adjectives/pronouns). Part of speech tags were assigned to the major category level. All single-character words/roots were filtered out, though none were present in this batch after processing.

Several words required splitting: 'በየሙያውና' into 'በየሙያው' and 'እና'; 'አካሂዷልን' into 'አካሂዷል' and 'ን'; 'ዘንካታነቱና' into 'ዘንካታነቱ' and 'እና'; 'ወደምትባልና' into 'ወደምትባል' and 'እና'; 'በጠጠሮችና' into 'በጠጠሮች' and 'እና'; and 'የፍቅና' into 'የፍቅር' and 'እና'. The single-character suffix 'ን' from 'አካሂዷልን' was discarded as it lacks independent dictionary meaning. Scholarly guesses were made to correct likely OCR errors: 'ሜኔዥ' was corrected to 'ሜኔጀር' (manager), 'ዕቃድ' to 'ዕቅድ' (plan), 'የፍቅና' to 'የፍቅር' (of love), and 'ሂሦችን' to 'ሂሶችን' (criticisms). All remaining words were lemmatized to their global citation form, translated with comprehensive synonyms, and assigned a major Part of Speech tag. All words included in the final dataset are at least two characters long.

The input batch was processed through 8 scholarly protocols. Several words were split: 'የሞጨሞጨችና' into 'የሞጨሞጨች' and 'ና'; 'ትጠፋም' into 'ትጠፋ' and 'ም'; 'ስታነሳና' into 'ስታነሳ' and 'ና'; 'ከሊቅም' into 'ሊቅ' and 'ም'; 'በወንዞቿና' into 'ወንዝ' and 'ና'; 'ወደቤታቸወ' into 'ቤት' and 'ወደ'; 'ከስምምነታቸውም' into 'ስምምነት' and 'ም'. Prepositions 'በ' and 'ከ' were discarded when they appeared as single-character prefixes (e.g., from 'በሌንኪጅ', 'ከትምክህተኝነት', 'በቅርጫፍ') as per Protocol 7. Scholarly Guesses were made for visual and morphological corrections: 'ዬነካ' was corrected to 'እየነካ' (touching); 'አረግራጊ' was interpreted as 'አርገብጋቢ' (shaker/vibrator) from the root 'ማርገብገብ' (to shake/vibrate); 'ወደቤታቸወ' had 'ቸወ' corrected to 'ቸው' for 'ቤታቸው' (their house); 'ሲዑል' was corrected to 'ሲዖል' (Sheol) for standard Amharic spelling; and 'ወደጁ' was corrected to 'ወዳጁ' (his friend/lover).

The input batch was processed through 8 scholarly protocols. Several words contained suffixes like 'ም' (and/also) and 'ና' (and), which were separated to identify the core word for lemmatization (e.g., 'ማስደነቅም' -> 'ማስደነቅ', 'የአሠሪውና' -> 'አሠሪ'). Scholarly guesses were made for 'ቤጉህዴፓን', identified as a proper noun/acronym, and 'እንደምሽግ', interpreted as 'ምሽግ' (trench/bunker) based on common usage. All roots and words were confirmed to be at least two characters long, adhering to the size constraint. No words were discarded as gibberish or unfixable.

The batch was processed through all 8 scholarly protocols. No words required splitting. The word 'ሙዪ' was discarded as it appears to be an unidentifiable foreign proper noun or a highly specific term without general Amharic dictionary value. Several words were identified as transliterations of foreign proper nouns or technical terms, including 'ዴያኒራ' (Deianira), 'ሆንዳይ' (Hyundai), 'ቮዳቴፎንስ' (Vodafone), 'ሴልሮንስ' (Celerons), and 'ዴኢታዎች' (data), and were treated as such with appropriate roots and synonyms.

Single-character functional morphemes like 'ና', 'ም', and 'ል' were discarded as per Protocol 7. Visual corrections were applied to 'አፍርካ' (corrected to 'አፍሪካ') and 'መልክእክት' (corrected to 'መልእክት'). A scholarly guess was made for 'በእንግሊዝ' from 'መልክእክትበእንግሊዝ', interpreting it as 'እንግሊዝኛ' (English language) for better lexicographical value. The word 'ላምፋ' was treated as 'ላም' with 'ፋ' being unfixable noise, and only 'ላም' was processed.
